factors.summary: The company has made strategic acquisitions that are expected to complement existing segments and contribute to future growth.

evidence.text: During the third quarter of 2017 , we acquired two businesses for total consideration of $802 million in cash, net of cash acquired, including the acquisition of the remaining 80% of a business in which we previously had an ownership interest. The acquisition of the remaining 80% interest also resulted in the revaluation of our prior interest, and we recorded a gain from acquisition of $15.3 million. The businesses acquired complement existing units of the Industrial Technology and Professional Instrumentation segments.

factors.summary: Order backlog is not explicitly addressed in the provided text.

evidence.text: No specific information on order backlog or book-to-bill ratios was found in the text.

factors.summary: The company has successfully raised prices, contributing to revenue growth.

evidence.text: Year-over-year price increases in the segment contributed 0.6% and 0.4% during the three and nine months ended September 29, 2017, respectively.

factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin segments, particularly in the Professional Instrumentation segment.

evidence.text: Operating profit margin increased 40 basis points during the three months ended September 29, 2017 as compared to the comparable period of 2016. Year-over-year operating profit margin comparisons were favorably impacted by higher 2017 sales volumes, incremental year-over-year cost savings, lower intangible asset amortization, and changes in currency exchange rates.

factors.summary: Unit volumes are growing, driven by increased demand across various product lines.

evidence.text: Year-over-year sales from existing businesses of field solutions products and services grew at a mid-single digit rate during both the three and nine months ended September 29, 2017 due to increased demand for industrial test equipment, network tools, and online condition-based monitoring equipment.

factors.summary: Input costs are manageable, with incremental cost savings offsetting any increases.

evidence.text: The year-over-year increase in cost of sales during the three months ended September 29, 2017 as compared to the comparable period in 2016 is due primarily to the impact of higher year-over-year sales volumes and changes in currency exchange rates partly offset by incremental year-over-year cost savings associated with restructuring and productivity improvement initiatives, and material cost and supply chain improvement actions.
evidence.subsection: COST OF SALES AND GROSS PROFIT

factors.summary: Labor costs are manageable, with no significant margin pressure noted.

evidence.text: R&D expenses (consisting principally of internal and contract engineering personnel costs) increased during both the three and nine months ended September 29, 2017 as compared to the comparable periods of 2016 due to incremental year-over-year investments in our product development initiatives.

factors.summary: Gross and operating margins are expanding, driven by higher sales volumes, cost savings, and favorable currency effects.

evidence.text: The year-over-year increase in gross profit (and the related 50 basis point increase in gross profit margin) during the three months ended September 29, 2017 as compared to the comparable period in 2016 is due primarily to the favorable impact of higher year-over-year sales volumes, incremental year-over-year cost savings associated with restructuring and productivity improvement initiatives, material cost and supply chain improvement actions, and changes in currency exchange rates.
evidence.subsection: COST OF SALES AND GROSS PROFIT

factors.summary: Restructuring and productivity initiatives are delivering measurable savings, contributing to margin expansion.

evidence.text: Year-over-year operating profit margin comparisons were favorably impacted by higher 2017 sales volumes, incremental year-over-year cost savings associated with restructuring and productivity improvement initiatives, lower year-over-year intangible asset amortization due to certain intangible assets, primarily in our Professional Instrumentation segment, being fully amortized, and changes in currency exchange rates.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Restructuring and productivity initiatives are delivering measurable savings, contributing to margin expansion.

factors.summary: Productivity is improving, driven by restructuring and efficiency initiatives.

evidence.text: The year-over-year increase in gross profit (and the related 50 basis point increase in gross profit margin) during the three months ended September 29, 2017 as compared to the comparable period in 2016 is due primarily to the favorable impact of higher year-over-year sales volumes, incremental year-over-year cost savings associated with restructuring and productivity improvement initiatives, material cost and supply chain improvement actions, and changes in currency exchange rates.
evidence.subsection: COST OF SALES AND GROSS PROFIT

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Productivity improvements driven by restructuring and efficiency initiatives are enhancing margins.

evidence.text: No specific mention of forward guidance changes in the provided text.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: No specific mention of changes in forward guidance, indicating stability but no clear direction.

factors.summary: Management is confident, with investments in growth initiatives and stable margins indicating confidence.

evidence.text: SG&A expenses increased during the three and nine months ended September 29, 2017 as compared to the comparable periods of 2016 due primarily to continued investments in our sales and marketing growth initiatives, increased acquisition-related transaction costs, and increased general and administrative costs required to operate as a stand-alone public company.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Management's confidence is evident through investments in growth initiatives and stable margins.

factors.summary: Near-term risks include unfavorable currency exchange rates and potential integration challenges from acquisitions.

evidence.text: Year-over-year operating profit margin comparisons were unfavorably impacted by acquisition related transaction costs and the incremental year-over-year net dilutive effect of acquired businesses.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Near-term risks include unfavorable currency exchange rates and potential integration challenges from acquisitions.

factors.summary: The company has a clear long-term strategic direction, focusing on growth through acquisitions and organic initiatives.

evidence.text: Sales from existing businesses in the segment’s Advanced Instrumentation & Solutions businesses grew at a mid-single digit rate during both the three and nine months ended September 29, 2017 as compared to the comparable periods of 2016.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Clear long-term strategic focus on growth through acquisitions and organic initiatives.

factors.summary: Key sensitivities include currency exchange rates, which have both favorable and unfavorable impacts on margins.

evidence.text: Changes in currency exchange rates impacted both sales and operating profit margins, with favorable effects in some periods and unfavorable in others.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Currency exchange rates have both favorable and unfavorable impacts on margins, indicating mixed sensitivity.

factors.summary: New product launches and integration of acquired businesses are expected to positively impact results.

evidence.text: Included in the 40 basis points increase in operating profit margin is the incremental year-over-year net dilutive effect of acquired businesses of 10 basis points.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: New product launches and integration of acquired businesses are expected to positively impact results.

factors.summary: Construction activity and infrastructure spending are driving demand in certain segments, particularly in industrial and transportation technologies.

evidence.text: Sales from existing businesses in the segment’s Transportation Technologies businesses grew at a low-single digit rate during the three months ended September 29, 2017 and at a mid-single digit rate during the nine months ended September 29, 2017, as compared to the comparable periods of 2016, due primarily to strong demand for dispensers and payment systems in both the United States and Europe.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Construction activity and infrastructure spending are driving demand in certain industrial and transportation technology segments.

factors.summary: Equipment replacement cycles are contributing to growth in certain segments, particularly in industrial and transportation technologies.

evidence.text: Year-over-year sales from existing businesses of product realization solutions grew at a mid-single digit rate during the three months ended September 29, 2017 driven primarily by continued growth in the semiconductor and consumer electronics end markets as well as increased demand for oscilloscopes and new product introductions.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Equipment replacement cycles are contributing to growth in industrial and transportation technologies.

factors.summary: Dealer/distributor inventory levels are healthy, supporting growing end-user demand.

evidence.text: Sales from existing businesses increased on a year-over-year basis in Europe and Latin America, partially offset by declines in the Asia-Pacific region during both the three and nine months ended September 29, 2017.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Healthy dealer/distributor inventory levels support growing end-user demand.

factors.summary: Aftermarket/service revenue is growing slower than new equipment sales, with the installed base driving some recurring revenue.

evidence.text: Year-over-year sales from existing businesses of product realization solutions grew at a mid-single digit rate during the three months ended September 29, 2017 driven primarily by continued growth in the semiconductor and consumer electronics end markets as well as increased demand for oscilloscopes and new product introductions.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Aftermarket/service revenue growth is slower than new equipment sales, indicating a mixed performance.

factors.summary: Overall economic conditions are stable, with no significant tailwinds or headwinds mentioned.

evidence.text: The effect of inflation on our sales and net earnings was not significant in the nine month period ended September 29, 2017.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Overall economic conditions are stable without significant tailwinds or headwinds.

factors.summary: Interest rate changes have a minimal impact, with stable interest costs indicating no significant benefit or harm.

evidence.text: Interest expense of $23 million and $68 million was recorded for the three and nine months ended September 29, 2017, respectively, compared to $23 million and $26 million for the three and nine months ended September 30, 2016, respectively.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Interest rate changes have minimal impact with stable interest costs.

factors.summary: Free cash flow generation has shown a negative trend, with a decrease in cash flows from operating activities and net cash provided by operating activities compared to the prior year periods.

evidence.text: Cash flows from operating activities decreased by $106 million, or approximately 13%, compared to the prior year period.
Net cash provided by operating activities decreased to $713.0 million in 2017 from $819.4 million in 2016.
evidence.subsection: Overview of Cash Flows and Liquidity

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Free cash flow has decreased significantly, indicating financial strain.

factors.summary: The company's capital expenditure outlook is mixed. While the investing activities section indicates a cautious approach with a decrease in capex, the operating profit margins section provides no specific information on future capex plans.

evidence.text: Capital expenditures decreased $2 million during the nine months ended September 29, 2017 as compared to the comparable period of 2016 due primarily to timing of these investments within the year.
No specific information on capital expenditure plans was found in the provided text.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Mixed outlook with decreased capex but no future plans info.

factors.summary: The company has demonstrated increased activity in acquisitions, with a notable rise in cash payments indicating a focus on growth and value creation. This trend is supported by both the investing activities and cash flows subsections, which highlight significant investments in business acquisitions.

evidence.text: Net cash used in investing activities increased $612 million during the nine months ended September 29, 2017 due primarily to business acquisitions completed during this period.
Cash paid for acquisitions increased to $802.1 million in 2017 from $190.6 million in 2016.
evidence.subsection: Overview of Cash Flows and Liquidity

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Increased acquisitions suggest growth and value creation.

factors.summary: The company's debt leverage has shown mixed trends, with an increase in debt levels and stable interest costs.

evidence.text: On August 24, 2017, we entered into a new term loan agreement that provides for a five-year ¥13.8 billion senior unsecured term facility... which yielded net proceeds of approximately $126 million.

Interest expense of $23 million and $68 million was recorded for the three and nine months ended September 29, 2017, respectively, compared to $23 million and $26 million for the three and nine months ended September 30, 2016, respectively.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Debt levels increased, but interest costs are stable.

factors.summary: The company has shown confidence in its financial position through increased dividends, though there is no specific mention of changes in buyback or dividend policies in the operating profit margins section.

evidence.text: Cash dividends of $73 million were paid to shareholders during the nine months ended September 29, 2017.
No specific information on shareholder returns was found in the provided text.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Increased dividends show confidence in financial health.

factors.summary: Demand trends are mixed across different business segments, with overall demand showing an increase but also exhibiting stability in certain areas.

evidence.text: While differences exist among our businesses, on an overall basis, demand for our products, software and services increased during the three months ended September 29, 2017 as compared to the comparable period of 2016 resulting in aggregate year-over-year total sales growth of 7.5% and sales growth from existing businesses of 4.8% .

Year-over-year sales from existing businesses of field solutions products and services grew at a mid-single digit rate during both the three and nine months ended September 29, 2017 due to increased demand for industrial test equipment, network tools, and online condition-based monitoring equipment.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Overall demand increased across most business segments.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is stable, with existing businesses contributing positively across segments. The growth is supported by investments in sales initiatives and new products, as well as increased demand in high-growth markets and stabilized market conditions.

evidence.text: Our continued investments in sales growth initiatives and new product introductions, as well as increased demand in high-growth markets and stabilization of market conditions in developed markets and other business-specific factors discussed below contributed to overall sales growth from existing businesses across the majority of our businesses in the period.

Sales from existing businesses in the segment’s Advanced Instrumentation & Solutions businesses grew at a mid-single digit rate during both the three and nine months ended September 29, 2017 as compared to the comparable periods of 2016.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Stable organic growth supported by investments and new products.

factors.summary: The end market momentum is mixed, with high-growth markets such as China and Western Europe experiencing strong demand, particularly in industrial, semiconductor, and consumer electronics sectors. However, growth in North America and the medical end market is slower, indicating some areas of weaker performance.

evidence.text: Year-over-year sales from existing businesses grew at a low-single digit rate in developed markets and at a low-double digit rate in high-growth markets during the three months ended September 29, 2017 as compared to the comparable 2016 period. Year-over-year sales from existing businesses grew at a rate in the mid-teens in China, at a high-single digit rate in Western Europe and at a low-single digit rate in North America during the three months ended September 29, 2017.

Increased year-over-year demand in the industrial end market was partly offset by lower demand in the medical end market. Geographically, sales from existing businesses increased on a year-over-year basis during both the three and nine months ended September 29, 2017, due primarily to growth in Asia and North America.
